name: egui
description: Immediate-mode, pure-Rust, easy to embed, widely used for tooling UIs

first-release.comment: First crates.io publish was version 0.1.2 on 2020-06-10.

maintenance-status.comment: 0.34.0 on 2026-03-26 and 0.34.1 on 2026-03-27; thousands of commits on main.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.x series) with breaking changes each minor release; README states 'in active development... lacks many features and the interfaces are still in flux.' Widely used in production but not API-stable.

corporate-backing.value: Rerun (rerun.io) — sponsors primary development by Emil Ernerfeldt

implementation.comment: No unsafe in egui itself; all core crates (egui, epaint, emath) are pure Rust. Renderer integrations may bring C deps via wgpu/glow.

rendering-mode.comment: README: 'Pure immediate mode: no callbacks' — UI rebuilt from state each frame.

reactivity-model.comment: Per RESEARCH.md guidance: egui is fundamentally immediate, but classified as retained-tree because state mutation happens via direct Ui method calls (no Message enum, no signals, no VDOM).

thread-model.comment: UI must run on the main thread (via eframe/winit). egui::Context uses an internal RwLock so widget code can read Context data from other threads, but rendering/event-loop is main-thread-only.

renderer.comment: Official integration crates: egui-wgpu (wgpu — default in eframe) and egui_glow (OpenGL/WebGL). egui itself is backend-agnostic, outputting triangle meshes, so third-party backends (e.g., Skia, software) exist.

gpu-acceleration.comment: Default rendering is GPU-accelerated via wgpu (or OpenGL via glow).

hidpi-support.comment: Automatic scaling via pixels_per_point; handled by egui-winit/eframe integration.

vector-rendering.comment: epaint provides vector primitives (shapes, paths, stroked/filled geometry). SVG image loading via egui_extras.

custom-shaders.comment: wgpu and glow backends expose paint callbacks for injecting custom render passes; see examples/custom_3d_glow.

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: Paint callbacks allow embedding wgpu/OpenGL viewports; examples/custom_3d_glow demonstrates this.

linux-wayland.comment: Wayland supported via winit; app-id setting and native Wayland surfaces work. Some edge cases (popups) historically rough.

ios.comment: No official iOS target in eframe. Community builds exist via cargo-mobile but are not maintained by the egui project; users seek up-to-date guides as of late 2024.

android.comment: Official hello_android example; eframe README lists Android as a supported native target.

web-wasm.comment: First-class web target via eframe WASM builds; live demo at egui.rs.

embedded-no-std.comment: egui depends on std (ahash, unicode-segmentation, etc.); no no_std support.

authoring-style.comment: Builder pattern with closure-based scoping (ui.horizontal(|ui| { ... })); no macro DSL or external markup.

hot-reload.comment: No dedicated hot-reload story. Users rely on generic Rust hot-reload tooling like dioxus-hot-reload or dexterous_developer if they want it.

layout-system.comment: Widget-driven flow layout via horizontal/vertical/columns; no flexbox or grid constraint solver. Manual positioning also available.

styling-approach.comment: Global theming via Context::set_style/set_visuals (Rust theme struct); per-widget overrides passed inline.

theme-switching.comment: Built-in dark/light themes, switchable at runtime via Context::set_visuals.

accessibility.comment: Optional AccessKit integration; native API support on Windows and macOS. Experimental built-in screen reader on web.

internationalization.comment: No built-in i18n: non-Latin scripts require user-supplied fonts via Context::set_fonts; no built-in RTL layout, bidi, or localization framework.

widget-breadth.comment: Core covers buttons, sliders, checkboxes, radio, text edit (multi-line, copy/paste, undo), color picker, windows, tooltips, collapsing headers, scroll areas, images. egui_extras adds Table and DatePickerButton. egui_plot (official) adds charts. Per RESEARCH guideline, this places egui at 3–4; assigning 4 for rich stdlib across first-party crates.

data-grid-table.comment: Virtualized Table widget via egui_extras (first-party, same repo).

rich-text-markdown.comment: RichText struct supports per-span fonts/colors/sizes in core. Markdown via egui_commonmark (third-party but widely used).

tree-outline.comment: No dedicated tree widget in core or egui_extras; CollapsingHeader can be nested manually to simulate one.

native-menus.comment: egui draws its own menu bar and context menus inside the canvas; no integration with platform-native menu bars (e.g., macOS top menu).

file-dialogs.comment: Not built into egui core; eframe README recommends rfd and egui ships an examples/file_dialog sample using it.

charts-plotting.comment: egui_plot (first-party, extracted from the main repo in 2024) provides line, bar, and box plots. Maintainer is seeking help as of 2026.

custom-widgets.comment: Widget trait is minimal (a single method returning Response). Immediate-mode means no lifecycle boilerplate; Response API gives input handling for free. Well-documented pattern.

async-runtime-compatible.comment: egui itself is sync and does not bundle or mandate any async runtime. Apps can freely drive any runtime alongside it (examples/external_eventloop_async demonstrates this); tagged sync-only because there is no first-class async integration in the framework.

system-tray.comment: Not built into egui or eframe; users integrate third-party crates like tray-icon.

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notification support in egui or eframe.

clipboard.comment: Context::copy_text/copy_image for writes; clipboard reads flow in via raw input events handled by eframe/egui-winit.

drag-and-drop.comment: File drag-and-drop delivered via raw input hovered_files/dropped_files; internal widget drag-and-drop API (dnd_drag_source/dnd_drop_zone) added in recent releases.

embedded-webview.comment: No embedded webview component; egui is a standalone renderer.

multi-window.comment: Multi-viewport API (examples/multiple_viewports, examples/serial_windows) added in 0.24 enables true OS-level child/parent windows on native.

binary-size.comment: Not independently measured in this research pass; known to vary significantly by backend (glow generally smaller than wgpu). Users typically report 5–15 MB stripped for minimal eframe apps on wgpu.

startup-time.comment: Not independently measured in this research pass. wgpu initialization is the usual bottleneck; glow backend starts faster.

memory-footprint.comment: Not independently measured in this research pass.

idle-redraw.comment: README: 'only repaints during interaction or animation, not during idle periods.' Despite being immediate-mode, egui does not run a continuous render loop.

dependency-count.comment: egui crate itself has ~13 direct deps. A typical eframe + wgpu app transitively pulls in ~150–200 crates, placing the overall footprint in the moderate band (closer to heavy with wgpu).

documentation-quality.comment: Good API docs on docs.rs with inline examples; live web demo serves as the practical tutorial. No dedicated book/guide. README is terse. 23 examples in the repo compensate.

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic builders, closure-scoped layouts, and a trivial Widget trait. Minor friction around persisting state across frames (via Ui memory or user structs) — an inherent cost of immediate-mode.

examples-count.comment: 23 official examples covering custom fonts, custom rendering, multi-viewport, screenshots, keyboard input, file dialogs, Android, etc.

interactive-playground.comment: Full egui demo app compiled to WASM and hosted on the official site; exercises most core widgets live.

ide-tooling.comment: No egui-specific editor tooling because there is no DSL — rust-analyzer handles everything generically.

dev-tools-inspector.comment: Built-in debug overlays: Context::set_debug_on_hover reveals widget rects/IDs on hover; Settings/Inspection/Memory windows accessible from the demo app.

github-stars.comment: Approx 28.8k stars as checked 2026-04-24; number stales quickly.

crates-io-downloads.comment: Not captured in this pass; crates.io page did not render via WebFetch. lib.rs reports >1.2M monthly downloads, implying all-time totals in the tens of millions.

last-release.comment: 0.34.1 patch release on 2026-03-27 (0.34.0 the day prior).

notable-production-users.value: Rerun (multimodal data visualization SDK); widely used for dev tools, game tooling, and debug UIs in Rust game engines (Bevy via bevy_egui)

community-size.comment: 28.8k GitHub stars, active Discord, hundreds of contributors, frequent community crates in the ecosystem.

ecosystem-crates.comment: First-party: egui_extras (Table, DatePicker, image loaders), egui_plot, egui-wgpu, egui_glow, egui-winit. Third-party: egui_commonmark (markdown), egui-phosphor (icons), egui_graphs, bevy_egui, egui-material3, and a community-maintained 3rd-party crates wiki.
